title: Palantir's Alex Karp criticizes token-based enterprise AI pricing
friendlyTitle: Palantir says AI token bills are a problem for businesses
summary: Palantir CEO Alex Karp argued that token-based pricing from major AI labs is frustrating enterprise customers and pushed instead for more control over compute, models, data and proprietary workflows.
friendlySummary: Palantir's CEO said companies do not want to keep paying rising token costs and want more control over their AI systems.
whyItMatters: Enterprise AI buyers are becoming more cost-sensitive as model use scales. The practical takeaway is to measure outcomes per workflow, not just model quality, and to compare closed APIs with open-weight or custom systems where control matters.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Businesses using AI need to watch cost closely. The cheapest-looking model is not always cheapest once everyone starts using it heavily.
